definition: A spongy, multi-layered epidermis covering the aerial roots of many epiphytic orchids that absorbs water and nutrients.
source citation: Pridgeon, A.M. (1987). The velamen and exodermis of orchid roots.

definition: the modified median petal of an orchid flower, often serving as a landing platform for pollinators.
source citation: Dressler, R.L. (1981). The Orchids: Natural History and Classification.

definition: A thickened, bulb-like stem segment used by many orchids for water and nutrient storage.
The central reproductive structure of an orchid, fusing stamen and pistil into a single organ.
